incomeTypes.label: Personlig indkomst (spekulation)
incomeTypes.description: Speculation gains/losses from selling or trading crypto (Statsskatteloven §§ 4-5). Not subject to AM-bidrag.

incomeTypes.label: Personlig indkomst (erhverv)
incomeTypes.description: Business income from crypto activities (mining, staking, airdrops at commercial scale). Subject to AM-bidrag.

notes: 2026 tax reform: Denmark moved from a 2-bracket to a 4-bracket income tax system (bundskat, mellemskat, topskat, toptopskat).
Crypto speculation gains are taxed as personlig indkomst under Statsskatteloven §§ 4-5.
FIFO is applied per asset (BTC and ETH are separate queues).
AM-bidrag (8%) does NOT apply to speculation gains — only to business (erhverv) income.
If mining/staking is at commercial scale (erhvervsmæssig virksomhed), AM-bidrag applies.
Losses are deductible but only at the municipal tax rate (~26%), not the full marginal rate.
Crypto-to-crypto swaps are taxable disposal events.
No holding period exemption exists in Denmark.
The personfradrag is shared across all income — if salary uses it, crypto gains are taxed from the first krone.

Bracket thresholds: mellemskat kicks in above 641,200 DKK, topskat above 777,900 DKK, toptopskat above 2,592,700 DKK. The skatteloft caps bundskat + mellemskat + topskat + kommuneskat at ~52%. Toptopskat (5%) is added on top of the skatteloft.
Combined marginal rates (approx.): ~37% base, ~44.5% with mellemskat, ~52% with topskat (skatteloft), ~57% with toptopskat.
From 2026, EU DAC8 directive requires crypto-asset service providers to report user transactions directly to Skattestyrelsen.
Market value at receipt becomes cost basis for mining/staking/airdrop income.
